prompt:
Ultra-realistic portrait of a beautiful Korean woman seated sideways on top of a wooden desk, legs crossed at the ankle and dangling freely, holding a small convenience store onigiri in both hands, mid-bite — eyes slightly upward, expression softly amused, utterly at ease. Casual and unposed, the energy of a candid moment. Camera at standing eye-level, slightly angled down toward her.
Wearing a cropped Y2K-aesthetic baby blue zip-up track jacket over a white graphic tee with Korean text print, paired with low-rise straight-leg light wash jeans with a slight fray at the hem. Platform chunky sneakers in white and blue. Butterfly hair clips gathering sections of her dark brown hair loosely. Small silver hoop earrings. A konbini plastic bag with snacks hanging off the corner of the desk. AirPods in, one dangling out.
The golden afternoon light softens here — the classroom is in a more relaxed late-afternoon phase. Light falls across the desk, illuminating the onigiri wrapper and her hands, catching the chrome of the track jacket zipper. The lively scene contrasts with the quiet, sunlit classroom stillness around her.
35mm analog film grain, Kodak Portra 400 — baby blue rendered in Portra's characteristic slightly faded softness, light wash denim warm and luminous. Hyper-realistic denim texture, food wrapper detail, hair clip glint. Shallow depth of field. Mood: carefree Gen-Z Y2K nostalgia, slice-of-life editorial, Korean youth culture candid.
